sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have a much clearer picture of the maze structure:
- White chamber 1: id=9, 25 pixels, boundary=[[14, 20], [14, 24], [18, 24], [18, 20]] - a 5x5 square at rows 14-18, cols 20-24
- White chamber 2: id=10, 331 pixels, boundary=[[14, 32], [14, 38], [38, 38], [38, 20], [26, 20], [26, 31], [25, 32]] - a large irregular white region
- Red path: id=7, 82 pixels, boundary traces a loop
The large white chamber (id=10) has boundary:
- [14, 32] to [14, 38]: top edge at row 14, cols 32-38
- [14, 38] to [38, 38]: right edge at col 38, rows 14-38
- [38, 38] to [38, 20]: bottom edge at row 38, cols 20-38
- [38, 20] to [26, 20]: left edge at col 20, rows 26-38
- [26, 20] to [26, 31]: top of inner section at row 26, cols 20-31
- [26, 31] to [25, 32]: diagonal corner
- [25, 32] to [14, 32]: left edge at col 32, rows 14-25
So the large white chamber is an irregular shape that spans rows 14-38, cols 20-38. It has a "notch" at rows 26-38, cols 20-31.
Now, the player is at rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (start position). The player can move LEFT and RIGHT in the top corridor. DOWN is blocked.
The white chambers are at rows 14-18. The player needs to move DOWN to enter them, but DOWN is blocked.
Wait, let me think about this differently. The player is a 5x5 hollow square. When the player moves DOWN by 6 rows, the new position would be rows 14-18. But the player's boundary is [[8, 14], [8, 18], [12, 18], [12, 14]], which is rows 8-12, cols 14-18. If the player moves DOWN by 6 rows, the new position would be rows 14-18, cols 14-18.
But rows 14-18, cols 14-18 is within the black maze structure, not the white chambers. The white chambers are at cols 20-24 and cols 32-38.
So the player needs to be at cols 20-24 or cols 32-38 to be above the white chambers. But the player can only be at cols 14-18, 20-24, 26-30, 32-36, or 38-42 (in 6-cell jumps).
Wait, the player is at cols 14-18 (5 cols). If the player moves RIGHT by 6 cols, the new position would be cols 20-24. If the player moves RIGHT again, the new position would be cols 26-30. And so on.
So the player positions are:
- Cols 14-18: start
- Cols 20-24: above white chamber 1
- Cols 26-30: between white chambers
- Cols 32-36: above white chamber 2 (partially)
- Cols 38-42: above red path
The white chamber 1 is at cols 20-24, which matches the player position cols 20-24. So if the player is at cols 20-24 and presses DOWN, the player would move to rows 14-18, cols 20-24, which is white chamber 1.
But I already tried this, and DOWN was blocked. Let me check if the player is actually at cols 20-24 when I pressed DOWN.
Actually, I moved LEFT 3 times from cols 38-42, which should put the player at cols 20-24. Then I pressed DOWN, and it was blocked.
Hmm, maybe the game checks if the destination is valid. The destination is rows 14-18, cols 20-24, which is the white chamber. But maybe the game considers the white chamber as "invalid" because it's not a black corridor.
Or maybe the game checks if the path is clear. The path from rows 8-12 to rows 14-18 goes through row 13, which is black. So the path is blocked.
But the player moves in 6-cell jumps, so it skips row 13. Maybe the game still checks row 13.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The player is a 5x5 hollow square. When the player moves DOWN, the game checks if the new position is valid. The new position is rows 14-18, cols 20-24. The white chamber is at rows 14-18, cols 20-24. So the new position is within the white chamber.
But maybe the game considers the white chamber as "invalid" because the player needs to be on black tiles. Or maybe the game considers the white chamber as "valid" and the player can enter it.
Let me try pressing DOWN from cols 20-24 again, but this time I'll check the animation frames to see if there's a subtle change.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the player position. The player boundary is [[8, 14], [8, 18], [12, 18], [12, 14]], which is rows 8-12, cols 14-18. But the player is a 5x5 hollow square, so the actual player cells are at rows 8-12 and cols 14-18.
When the player moves DOWN by 6 rows, the new position would be rows 14-18, cols 14-18. But the white chamber 1 is at rows 14-18, cols 20-24. So the player would not be in the white chamber if it moves DOWN from cols 14-18.
So the player needs to be at cols 20-24 to be above the white chamber 1. Let me move RIGHT once from the start position (cols 14-18) to reach cols 20-24, and then press DOWN.

Plan: Move RIGHT to cols 20-24 (above white chamber 1) and try DOWN. If still blocked, try other positions.
